steps.title: Establish similar triangles by AA
steps.content: $\overline{LM} \parallel \overline{PQ}$, so $\angle MLR = \angle PQR$ (alternate interior angles) and $\angle LRM = \angle PRQ$ (vertical angles). By AA, $\triangle LMR \sim \triangle PQR$.

steps.title: Scale ratio, then area ratio
steps.content: Corresponding sides: $MR = 8$, $PR = 14$. Length scale factor $k = \dfrac{14}{8} = \dfrac{7}{4}$, so area scales by $k^2 = \dfrac{49}{16}$.

steps.title: Compute area of $\triangle PQR$
steps.content:
$$\text{Area}(\triangle PQR) = 36 \times \frac{49}{16} = \frac{1764}{16} = \frac{441}{4}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
